title: Jeedai
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
{{Citation}}
{{Quote|We have found a new hope, and the word for that new hope is ''Jeedai''.|[[Vuurok I'pan]] to [[Nom Anor]]|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]''</ref>}}
'''''"Jeedai"''''' was a word for [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] commonly used by various [[Species/Legends|species]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], most notably during the [[Jedi Civil War]] and the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].

''Jeedai'' was the word for Jedi in [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]],<ref name="GPBTG">[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']]</ref> and was also commonly used by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. A notable exception among the Yuuzhan Vong was [[Nom Anor]], who used the word "Jedi" instead of ''Jeedai'', indicating the time he had spent in the galaxy prior to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. In other cases, ''Jeedai'' may have been intended as an insult, or possibly the equivalent of a regional inflection. The [[Morgukai]] warriors [[Bok]] and [[Tsyr]] referred to Jedi as ''Jeedai''.

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', [[Hanharr]]'s subtitle also uses the word "Jeedai" instead of "Jedi" but since he cannot pronounce words in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], it's only an implication that he pronounces the word in a different manner than any other [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]. [[Vossk]] also uses it in ''Knights of the Old Republic II''.

Various [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] in ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Jedi Alliance]]'' call the Jedi "Jeedai".

In first [[Marvel Comics]] in French, "Jeedaï" or "Djeedaï" are used to translate [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], as other names, in a more French line.
However, this translation is no longer used in their current [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]].

{{Stub}}
